review_comment:
For the life of me I couldn't figure out why appending SVG nodes containing `image` elements doesn't render the image (or set the dimensions) correctly. Repro: https://codesandbox.io/s/beautiful-black-9orux?file=/src/index.js (note: replacing `image` with `img` element — which isn't valid HTML inside `<svg>` AFAIK — does work in [CSB](https://codesandbox.io/s/awesome-fast-tw2tn?file=/src/index.js), but not in Excalidraw for some reason). Reproduces in Chrome and FF (haven't checked Safari).

Using `innerHTML` setter works.

I don't think we're subject to XSS as the output of the SVG should be safe (note: we're already using `outerHTML` when saving to `.svg` file). If that's a concern, we'll need to sanitize at SVG generation.

Yeah `img` is not a valid tag, for ref [docs]( https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Element/svg#usage_notes)
I also checked this and it's weird that via JS it's not working, however, if I hardcode the data URL in the image tag it works fine
